records.prompt_text: add a --verbose flag to this CLI and wire it into the existing logger
records.judge_reason: Adding a CLI flag and wiring it into an existing logger requires a small but concrete code edit (argument parsing + logger configuration), making this an S2 task, not a trivial S0 lookup.

records.prompt_text: write a pytest unit test for the Tier enum parser
records.judge_reason: Writing a pytest unit test for an enum parser requires generating a complete test file with multiple test cases, imports, and assertions — this is a single-file code generation task appropriate for S2, not a trivial S0 lookup.

records.prompt_text: add retry logic with exponential backoff to the HTTP client wrapper
records.judge_reason: Adding retry logic with exponential backoff requires multi-step implementation including retry loop, backoff calculation, error handling, and integration with existing HTTP client wrapper — easily S3 complexity, not a trivial S0 task.

records.prompt_text: diagnose why the brain classifier is routing S1 for prompts that should be S3 — look at signal weights and recommend adjustments
records.judge_reason: Diagnosing systematic routing failures, analyzing signal weights, and recommending calibration adjustments requires multi-step reasoning and cross-component analysis — clearly S3 complexity, not a simple S1 lookup.

records.prompt_text: implement a cost tracker that reads decisions.jsonl, aggregates by model and week, and outputs deltas as JSON
records.judge_reason: This task requires multi-step implementation: file I/O parsing JSONL, date aggregation logic by week, grouping by model, computing deltas, and JSON output — easily 20-50 lines of non-trivial code requiring S3.

records.prompt_text: add an async fallback chain to the advisor wrapper: retry on 429, escalate to Opus on repeated failure, log every transition
records.judge_reason: This task requires implementing multi-step async logic (retry on 429, escalation to Opus, transition logging) across a wrapper function, which is S3-level work involving dependency chains and 10-50 lines of code — routing to S0 is catastrophically wrong.

records.prompt_text: build a tokenizer for routing_manifest.toml that strips comments and validates section structure against a schema
records.judge_reason: Building a tokenizer with comment stripping and schema validation against a TOML manifest requires significant design work, multiple components (lexer, validator, schema definition), and cross-cutting logic that far exceeds a simple single-file edit.

records.prompt_text: wire brain_cli into PreCompact events and test the end-to-end output logging path
records.judge_reason: This task requires cross-file integration work (wiring CLI into event system), architectural understanding of the event pipeline, and end-to-end testing — far beyond trivial S0 scope.

records.prompt_text: design Homer's MUSES parallel dispatch from scratch — how workers share state, handle partial failures, and write back to VAULT atomically without race conditions
records.judge_reason: This is a complex architecture design question involving concurrent systems, shared state management, partial failure handling, and atomic write-back patterns — requiring deep multi-component reasoning well beyond S1's short Q&A scope.

records.prompt_text: refactor severity_classifier to support a hybrid trained-classifier + rule-based fallback with graceful degradation semantics
records.judge_reason: This is a complex architectural refactor requiring cross-file changes, hybrid system design, fallback logic implementation, and graceful degradation semantics — far beyond S1's capability for short Q&A.

records.prompt_text: my AnimBP is T-posing only when the movement component is replicating — trace the root cause across blueprint, skeletal mesh, and replication graph
records.judge_reason: This is a complex multi-system debugging task spanning Blueprint animation, skeletal mesh configuration, and replication graph interactions requiring deep cross-system analysis — catastrophically underrouted to S0 instead of S4.

records.prompt_text: audit the entire hook pipeline for compaction safety and design a fix that preserves telemetry across /compact without manual intervention
records.judge_reason: Auditing an entire hook pipeline for compaction safety, then designing a fix that preserves telemetry across context compaction requires deep architectural analysis, cross-system understanding, and multi-component design — this is S4-level work, not a single-file S2 patch.

records.prompt_text: design a tiered memory system implementing Letta's Core/Recall/Archival on top of existing MEMORY.md files without adding a vector database
records.judge_reason: Designing a tiered memory architecture (Core/Recall/Archival) with custom retrieval mechanisms without vector DB requires cross-file architectural design, novel system design decisions, and significant multi-component implementation — clearly S4 complexity, not a simple single-file edit.

records.prompt_text: review every skill in ~/.claude/skills, identify pattern drift against shared protocols, and produce per-skill specific edit proposals with reasoning
records.judge_reason: This task requires reading and analyzing multiple files across a directory, cross-referencing each against shared protocols, identifying pattern drift across all of them, and producing detailed per-skill edit proposals with reasoning — a classic long-context multi-file synthesis task requiring S4/S5, not a single-file S2 edit.

records.prompt_text: overnight: ingest 200 decisions from decisions.jsonl, cluster by failure mode, generate synthetic training data for a DSPy classifier, and compile it into an exportable prompt
records.judge_reason: This is a long-running multi-stage pipeline (ingest 200 records, cluster, generate synthetic data, compile prompts) requiring extensive context and overnight processing — clearly S5, not trivial S0.

records.prompt_text: read every file under Desktop/atelier, generate a feature-parity PR for Ink Finder that reuses Atelier's theme system, marketplace UI, and inbox pattern, with passing tests
records.judge_reason: This task requires reading an entire codebase, synthesizing cross-file architecture patterns, generating a large multi-file PR with feature parity, and writing passing tests — a classic S5 long-context, production-scale task that S2 cannot handle.

records.prompt_text: cat README.md
records.judge_reason: Displaying a file's contents is a trivial operation requiring no reasoning, just a single command output — S0 would suffice.

records.prompt_text: what does this regex do: ^\d{3}-\d{4}$
records.judge_reason: Explaining a regex pattern requires 1-2 sentences of description (matches 3 digits, hyphen, 4 digits anchored at start/end), making S1 more appropriate than S0, though S0 is adjacent.

records.prompt_text: rename variable foo to user_count in this function
records.judge_reason: Renaming a variable in a function is a single-file code edit that fits S2, though S1 is only one tier off and may be usable for a very small/simple function.

records.prompt_text: convert this JSON key from camelCase to snake_case
records.judge_reason: Converting a JSON key from camelCase to snake_case is a simple one-line transformation but requires producing a short output string, making S1 slightly more appropriate than S0, though S0 is adjacent and usable.

records.prompt_text: refactor this for-loop into a list comprehension
records.judge_reason: Refactoring a for-loop into a list comprehension is a small code edit task that fits S2, though S1 is adjacent and usable for very simple cases.

records.prompt_text: list files here
records.judge_reason: A simple directory listing request requires no reasoning, just a trivial one-line command response.

records.prompt_text: what day is it
records.judge_reason: Trivial question requiring a one-word or short answer, perfectly matched to S0.

records.prompt_text: print hello
records.judge_reason: Trivial one-line print statement requires no reasoning or complexity.

records.prompt_text: yes or no
records.judge_reason: A bare 'yes or no' prompt is trivially simple, requiring minimal processing, perfectly matching S0.

records.prompt_text: show the git log for main
records.judge_reason: A simple git log command request is a trivial one-line answer requiring no reasoning.

records.prompt_text: what port does PostgreSQL use by default
records.judge_reason: This is a trivial factual lookup with a one-word/number answer (5432) requiring no reasoning.

records.prompt_text: fix the off-by-one error in parse_range() where the max index isn't included
records.judge_reason: A focused single-function bug fix for an off-by-one error is a standard small code change requiring a 1-3 line patch, well-suited for S2.

records.prompt_text: read the entire Sworder Bible (108k lines), ue-knowledge (46k lines), and GDD — cross-reference every combat rule and build a unified decision table of canon sources with line-number receipts
records.judge_reason: This task requires ingesting and cross-referencing 150k+ lines across multiple large documents to synthesize a unified decision table, which demands maximum context window and long-form synthesis capabilities squarely in S5 territory.

records.prompt_text: full architecture review of Toke — Brain v2.3, Homer L0-L7, VAULT, Sybil, Muses — identify every inconsistency and produce a v3.0 redesign with migration plan
records.judge_reason: Full architecture review across multiple complex systems (Brain, Homer, VAULT, Sybil, Muses) with inconsistency identification and a v3.0 redesign plus migration plan is exactly the kind of long-context, multi-system synthesis requiring maximum context and compute that S5 is designed for.
